ps140: 1, Podoben “The joy of the heavenly ranks…”

The great Paul, the sun of the earth, emits you, O blessed and divinely wise one,* upon the world like a radiant beam,* to enlighten with the splendid radiant splendour of Your words* those who languished of old in the night of ignorance.

Your pure heart, O Aquila, of godly eloquence,* richly illumined with the brilliant rays of the divine Spirit,* was shown to be truly luminous,* and destroyed the cruel darkness of the heathen* with the grace of God.

They who have recourse to your divine temple* and entreat your therein with faith, O Aquila,* are delivered from all manner of infirmities, from tribulations and sorrows,* by your sacred supplications* and intercessions before God, O sacred one.
4, Podoben “As one valiant among the martyrs…”

With mature mind but a child’s body, O martyr Cyricus,* with manly wisdom you put to shame the infantile tyrant.* Therefore, grievously torturing you at his tribunal,* he imposed death upon you* who were mindful of the life which grows not old, into which you have entered,* drenched forever with blood unstaunched.

O glorious Julitta, with spiritual outpourings you brought forth Cyricus* who sprang forth from your womb like a fruitful vine,* and is truly slain and trodden out in the tyrant’s wine-press.* Pouring forth the wine of compunction with him,* gladden the hearts of those who keep your memory with faith.

Having come close to wounds, O right wondrous one,* you likewise truly endured the convulsion of your body;* and beholding your son’s end with your own eyes,* you passed through a double torture, O Julitta.* Therefore, the Judge of the contest bestows twofold crowns upon you,* omnipotently granting the victory to those who contend.

Come, all of you, and behold a sight strange and all-glorious!* Who has ever seen a child of three years putting a tyrant to shame?* O, the wonder!* He drank of his mother’s milk and cried out to her who nourished him:* Fear not the tortures of the cruel ruler of this world, O my mother,* for Christ is the strength of those who believe on Him!
stavrotheotokion: Your pure Virgin Mother, beholding the most iniquitous people* who unjustly nailed You to the Tree,* was wounded within, as Symeon foretold.
aposticha: A child of three years proclaimed the Trinity;* he who was still nursing on milk rendered his own mother steadfast, saying:* Cease shedding your tears, O my mother!* The Creator is watching from on high and will save our souls!
stavrotheotokion_aposticha: “I cannot bear to see You, O my Child,* who gives vigilance to all, asleep upon the Tree.* that You may bestow divine and saving wakefulness* upon those who of old fell into the sleep of perdition* by eating the fruit of disobedience!” the Virgin said, weeping.* We magnify her.
